title: Reconcile design-tokens.md <-> tokens.css AND fix WCAG contrast on ink-faint/ink-ghost

findings.description:
design-tokens.md is declared as the contract that tokens.css implements, and explicitly states 'If tokens.css and this file disagree, fix tokens.css to match this contract -- not the other way around.' Several values diverge:

- Light --bg-paper: doc says #ece7de, tokens.css has #f6f3ed (doc's --bg-paper value is actually at --bg-page in tokens.css)
- Light --bg-card-paper: doc says #f6f3ed, tokens.css uses --bg-card: #fdfcf9 (mismatch)
- Light --bg-page: doc says #fdfcf9, tokens.css has #ece7de (these are swapped vs doc)
- Light --ink: doc says #171513, tokens.css has #1c1814
- Light --ink-secondary: doc says #3c3a36, tokens.css has #4a4139
- Light --ink-muted: doc says #6a655c, tokens.css has #887d6e
- Light --ink-faint: doc says #9a948a, tokens.css has #beb3a2
- Light --ink-ghost: doc says #bdb6a9, tokens.css has #d5cdc0
- Light --border-light: doc says #e6e2d8, tokens.css has #e4ddd2
- Light --border-default: doc says #d6d1c4, tokens.css has #d5cdc0
- Light --accent-text: doc says #2c4360, tokens.css has var(--accent)
- Light --danger: doc says #a03821, tokens.css uses --error: #a84040
- Dark mode values also have multiple mismatches.

The naming scheme also diverges: doc uses --bg-paper/--bg-card-paper/--bg-page, while tokens.css uses --bg-page/--bg-paper/--bg-card.
findings.suggestedFix: Audit each divergence: if tokens.css was tuned deliberately for visual quality, update design-tokens.md to match. If tokens.css drifted accidentally, fix tokens.css to match the doc. Also reconcile the naming scheme (--bg-paper vs --bg-card-paper vs --bg-page).

findings.description: Computed contrast ratios: light mode ink-faint (#beb3a2) on bg-paper (#f6f3ed) = 1.87:1, ink-ghost (#d5cdc0) on bg-paper = 1.42:1. Dark mode ink-faint (#4f4639) on bg-paper (#191614) = 1.94:1, ink-muted (#6a6055) on bg-paper = 2.93:1. WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text. These tokens are used for metadata, timestamps, epigraphs, build versions, and mono kickers -- all small text that falls well below the minimum. The dark mode ink-muted at 2.93:1 also fails AA for the 10-11px mono text it is frequently used at (sidebar status, filter chips, section labels).
findings.suggestedFix: Adjust ink-faint in light mode from #beb3a2 to approximately #8a8070 (achieving ~3.5:1 against bg-paper) and in dark mode from #4f4639 to approximately #7a6d5e. Adjust ink-ghost to be reserved only for decorative, non-text elements (aria-hidden borders, dividers) -- audit all current usages and promote text usages to ink-faint or ink-muted. Consider adding a design-tokens.md note that ink-ghost must never carry readable text.
findings.codeSnippet:
--ink-faint: #beb3a2; /* 1.87:1 contrast on bg-paper */
--ink-ghost: #d5cdc0; /* 1.42:1 contrast on bg-paper */
